page.content: In examining the relationship between β-blocker use and cardiovascular complications among patients with acute coronary syndrome, the data presented in the accompanying table provides critical insights. The analysis includes a comparative assessment of patients treated with β-blockers (N = 205) versus those who did not receive this intervention (N = 315). Notably, the percentage of patients presenting with acute coronary syndrome was 0.5% in the β-blocker group, while the control group exhibited no cases, indicating a potential protective effect of β-blockers. Furthermore, the table outlines the distribution of cardiovascular complications across different sensor types, emphasizing the importance of sensor signal drift as a variable in the study.

page.content: The p value associated with the comparison of cardiovascular complications further elucidates the statistical significance of these findings. Overall, the data suggests that β-blockers may play a vital role in mitigating the risk of acute coronary syndrome, warranting further investigation into their efficacy and mechanisms of action in this patient population.
